data.art_direction_summary: Everyday Indian life where a good meal quietly arrives between the demands of the day. Not aspirational studio kitchens, not glossy delivery ads — real desks, real flats, real light. The mood is relief and calm control: someone busy who is, for once, well fed and within budget.
data.subject: Metro Indian tech professionals (26-35) and macro-trackers in their real contexts — a home desk at 1pm, a couch at 8pm, a phone showing a one-tap confirm, a delivery bag set down beside a laptop.
framing: Human hands and phone in frame; the app's one-tap moment visible but not screen-hero.
A delivered meal in its real container or plated simply, mid-day, beside work.
Over-the-shoulder of the plan being approved on a Sunday evening.
Candid, slightly imperfect crops — lived-in, not staged.
data.lighting: Natural daylight from a window; soft, warm, directional. Late-morning or golden-hour indoor light. No hard flash, no cold office fluorescents, no studio ring-light gloss.
data.palette: Warm cream and wood tones, saffron and green accents from the food and app; muted, desaturated surroundings so the food and the calm read first. Ripe tomato appears only as a real food colour or the confirm button on screen.
composition_rules: Real portions — a normal lunch, not a banquet.
Honest containers — delivery boxes and everyday crockery are welcome, not hidden.
Negative space and breathing room; never cluttered.
The phone/app is a supporting character, never a hard product-shot.
data.wardrobe_and_context: Casual-professional Indian everyday wear; flats and shared apartments, WFH desks, small kitchens, office pantries in Bengaluru/Hyderabad/Pune/Gurgaon.
data.diversity: Range of skin tones, genders, body types, and home types across metro India; no idealised or 'fitness-model' bodies; represent both 'Deadline Deepak' and 'Macro Meera' honestly.
avoid: Glossy fast-food gloss, dripping cheese-pull hero shots, artificial garnish.
Aspirational marble studio kitchens with no one in them.
Diet-app clichés — measuring tapes, before/after bodies, scales, 'guilt' imagery.
Perfectly styled flat-lays that look like an ad; keep it real.

sample_shot_list.title: The 1pm confirm
sample_shot_list.scene: A developer at a home desk glances at their phone mid-task; the screen shows 'Grilled chicken bowl, ₹240, arrives ~12:35 — confirm?'. Half-open laptop, warm window light, an empty cup. Relief, not excitement.

sample_shot_list.title: Meal arrives between meetings
sample_shot_list.scene: A delivery bag just set down beside a laptop with a call on screen; a hand reaching for it. The meal is the reward for staying in flow.

sample_shot_list.title: Sunday plan approval
sample_shot_list.scene: Over-the-shoulder, evening, someone on a couch scrolling next week's plan on the app — a calm review, tea in hand.

sample_shot_list.title: Honest lunch, plated simply
sample_shot_list.scene: A real rajma chawal or chicken bowl in everyday crockery on a wooden desk, top-down, natural light, a macro chip subtly visible on the phone beside it.

sample_shot_list.title: End-of-month, under cap
sample_shot_list.scene: A quiet moment: the app's spend ledger showing 'stayed under ₹9,000', a small satisfied smile, no fanfare.

sample_shot_list.title: Macro Meera's desk
sample_shot_list.scene: A fitness-minded professional post-workout at a standing desk; a high-protein delivered meal and a water bottle; the macro chip in view. Capable, not obsessive.
